    ⚡ Cloudflare Worker URL

    A Cloudflare Worker acts as a CORS proxy for features that can't be called directly from the browser (pollen data, NVD API token authentication, RSS feeds). Deploy the worker script to your free Cloudflare account and paste the URL here. The worker script is available at github.com/badbox29/personal_dashboard.

    Applies to all features that use the worker — pollen data, NVD/CVE requests, and RSS feeds. Individual widgets can override this with their own URL in their settings.

    Used by the URL Monitor widget to check internal services, HTTP endpoints, and hosts with self-signed certificates. Run the companion local-proxy-server.js on any always-on machine on your network. Leave blank until you have a local proxy deployed.

    Per-widget overrides: The Pollen (in Weather settings), RSS Feed widget, and CVE Bulletin widget each have their own optional Worker URL field. If filled in, that widget uses its own URL instead of this global one. Leave them blank to use this global setting.

    Syncs your dashboard state across multiple browsers via your Worker's KV storage. Each user gets a unique sync token. To set up a new browser, follow the steps below in order.

    Step 1 — Enter and save your Worker URL above if you haven't already. KV sync routes through the same worker.

    Step 2 — Your sync token is your identity. On your primary browser, copy it and keep it somewhere safe. On a new browser, use Enter Token to paste the token from your primary browser so both devices share the same KV namespace. Your token is also included automatically in any JSON export.

    Reset generates a new token and permanently disconnects this browser from its current KV data.

    Step 3 — On a new browser, after entering the token, click Pull from KV to immediately download your saved state. On your primary browser, use Push to KV to force an immediate upload of your current state.

    Step 4 — Once your state is confirmed, enable automatic sync. When active, any dashboard change will be pushed to KV within 5 seconds and pulled automatically on every page load.

    Enable automatic KV sync

    📑 Import Browser Bookmarks

    Export your bookmarks from your browser first:
    Chrome/Edge: Bookmarks menu → Bookmark manager → ⋮ → Export bookmarks
    Firefox: Bookmarks → Manage bookmarks → Import and Backup → Export to HTML

    No file chosen

    ⚠ Lo‑fi Audio in Microsoft Edge

    Edge blocks audio from YouTube iframes by default due to its strict autoplay policy. Firefox and Chrome don't have this restriction. To fix it in Edge:

    1. Click the link below to open Edge's Media Autoplay settings
    2. Under Allow, click Add
    3. Enter your page's address — e.g. http://localhost:8080 or your NAS IP such as http://10.163.1.236
    4. Click Add, then reload this page

    Note: edge:// links must be opened directly in Edge's address bar — they cannot be clicked from within a web page. Copy the address below and paste it into Edge's address bar.

    edge://settings/content/mediaAutoplay

    Weather Locations

    minutes ✓ Saved

    Minimum 5 minutes. If tiles show "Loading…" on startup they will recover at the next refresh cycle, or reload the page.

    Tree, grass, and weed pollen levels can be shown on each weather tile. Pollen data requires a paid third-party API subscription — pricing varies by provider. Your key is stored locally and included in exported settings files. Note that some providers may also require a CORS proxy to work from a browser — use the HTTP Proxy setting in the URL Monitor section if needed.

    A free Cloudflare Worker can act as a CORS proxy for pollen requests. Get the worker script from github.com/badbox29/personal_dashboard, deploy it to your Cloudflare account, and paste your worker URL above.
